operating_state: Worldwide sales increased 4.3% to $88.8 billion driven by 5.9% volume growth offset by 1.6% negative currency impact. Innovative Medicine grew with Oncology surging 17.7% led by DARZALEX and ERLEADA gains while Immunology declined due to STELARA biosimilar competition in Europe. MedTech increased 4.8% with Cardiovascular franchise delivering 21.4% growth from Shockwave acquisition, while Surgery declined due to China volume-based procurement pressures.
cost_structure: Gross margin improved as percentage of sales driven by lower COVID-19 vaccine exit costs and reduced restructuring charges, partially offset by Shockwave inventory step-up. Research and development expenses rose to 19.4% of sales from 17.7% reflecting acquired in-process development for NM26 bispecific antibody rights and V-Wave acquisition. Selling and administrative expenses increased due to heightened commercial investment while currency headwinds reduced consolidated margins.
material_events: Completed Shockwave Medical acquisition for $13.1 billion funded through senior unsecured notes issuance, contributing 1.5% positive MedTech impact. Divested remaining Kenvue stake through debt-for-equity exchange resulting in $0.4 billion loss. Increased dividend for 62nd consecutive year to $4.91 per share and completed February 2025 debt offerings totaling $5.0 billion USD and €4.0 billion EUR notes.
macro_exposures: China volume-based procurement continues impacting Surgery, Orthopaedics, and Vision segments with ongoing pricing pressures. Currency translation effects reduced growth with 1% USD change potentially affecting foreign sales by $0.4 billion. Pillar Two minimum tax legislation implemented across multiple jurisdictions with less than 1.0% impact to effective tax rate, expected up to 1.0% impact in 2025.
forward_outlook: Management expects continued STELARA biosimilar launches in Europe and United States during 2025 impacting Immunology performance. Capital allocation priorities include maintaining dividend growth, strategic acquisitions, and debt service including $2.5 billion TCJA payments due 2025. Company anticipates operating cash flows and existing credit facilities will provide sufficient liquidity for operations and remaining opioid settlement payments plus talc matter reserves.
uncertainty_profile: Talc litigation reserves of $11.6 billion remain subject to final settlement terms and court approvals with thousands of plaintiffs seeking indeterminate damages. Patent challenges through IPR proceedings and generic competition threaten key pharmaceutical exclusivity periods. IRA Medicare Drug Price Negotiation Program implementation uncertainty persists with Janssen's constitutional challenge under appeal, while cybersecurity risks remain elevated due to geopolitical tensions.
